page: Amazon Web Services r8gn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 16 dedicated physical cores based on the ARMv9 arm64 architecture, specifically the Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz, with a single thread per core. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Network connectivity is a key feature of this instance, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the r8gn.4xlarge demonstrates exceptional memory performance, achieving a top-tier rating in the Passmark memory score and strong results in large-block memory bandwidth tests, with read speeds of 285.68 GB/sec and write speeds of 134.17 GB/sec. Multi-threaded decompression and database operations also rank in the top 25% of tested hardware. However, CPU-bound tasks such as single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests perform in the average tier, and large-scale LLM inference tasks show poor performance, with text generation for a 70B model yielding only 2.39 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture and high memory density provide a favorable resource-to-overhead ratio for memory-bound workloads without the added expense of local NVMe storage or discrete GPUs. This makes the r8gn.4xlarge highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, including large-scale database operations, caching layers, multi-threaded data decompression, and text processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on the 2.8 GHz ARMv9 arm64 architecture (AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2). It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance and strong database operations, alongside average general CPU performance. The r8gn.4xlarge is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as database management, caching, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated ARMv9 cores, 128 GB of RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-performance database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 16 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring high-bandwidth memory architecture and dedicated physical cores for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps via the Nitro hypervisor
Achieves top-tier memory performance and strong database operation benchmarks
Optimized for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers, and database management
